title: Thinking Words vs. Doing Words
description: Learn which words describe actions and which words describe feelings or facts.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Thinking Words vs. Doing Words

In English, we have two types of words for what we do: **Doing Words** and **Thinking Words**.

### 1. Doing Words (Action Verbs)

Doing words describe something you can see. You can use the **-ing** form for these words when they are happening now.

> **Examples:**
> - **Eat**: I am eating my lunch now.
> - **Read**: You are reading a book.
> - **Walk**: The students are walking to class.

### 2. Thinking Words (State Verbs)

Thinking words describe feelings, opinions, or facts. You **cannot** see these actions. We almost never use **-ing** for these words, even if they are true now.

#### Categories of Thinking Words:

**Mind (Thinking)**
- **Understand**: I understand the homework. (Not: I am understanding)
- **Believe**: I believe you.
- **Know**: He knows my name.

**Feelings**
- **Like**: I like this school.
- **Love**: She loves coffee.
- **Want**: They want a break.

**Having things**
- **Have**: I have a red pen. (Not: I am having a red pen)
- **Own**: The school owns three buses.
- **Need**: You need a pencil.

### Why does it matter?

If you use **-ing** with a thinking word, it sounds strange to native speakers. 

- **Correct**: I **know** the answer.
- **Incorrect**: I am knowing the answer.

---

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Choose the best word to finish the sentence about campus life.

questions.question: The science book _______ three long chapters.
The teacher _______ the students' names now.
I _______ this new computer lab very much.
The library _______ many old books.
The student _______ the question from the teacher.
My friends _______ to go to the cafe after class.
Look! The students _______ to the big hall.
The school lunch _______ many vegetables.
I _______ you are right about the test date.
Right now, the professor _______ a map on the wall.
The math class _______ very difficult today.
We _______ more time to finish the project.
My roommate _______ a new blue bicycle.
The cafeteria _______ good today.
Listen! The students _______ the school song.
I _______ my keys for the locker.
The student _______ the answer to the first question.
They _______ a new football team at school.
The small bag _______ some pens and paper.
I _______ very happy in this English class.
The boy _______ a heavy bag to the bus stop.
We _______ tea more than coffee.
The computer _______ to my brother.
The girl _______ on a chair near the window.
I _______ why he is angry.
The university _______ a large park.
He _______ chocolate cake.
The children _______ in the garden.
The new student _______ very kind.
I _______ to buy a new dictionary.
